headline: World's Oldest Tortoise Caught in Viral Crypto Death Scam

intro.script_text: The world's oldest living land animal, Jonathan the Seychelles giant tortoise, has been caught up in a viral crypto death scam. A fake X account posing as his vet sparked global false reports of his death while soliciting cryptocurrency donations.
intro.visual_description: Aerial shot of Saint Helena island with the governor's mansion and lush greenery. The camera pans over to Jonathan's enclosure, showing the tortoise sleeping under a tree. Soft focus and warm lighting create a sense of serenity. The pace is slow and gentle, building anticipation for the reveal.

key_points.script_text: The fake X account claimed Jonathan had passed away peacefully on Saint Helena, but checks by the Guardian revealed the account was based in Brazil. The real vet said the scam was not an April fool's joke and was a con.
key_points.visual_description: Close-up shot of a computer screen displaying the fake X account's post. The text is bold and attention-grabbing, with a red 'X' symbol overlaid on top. The background is dark and ominous, setting the tone for the scam. The pace is fast and urgent, conveying the sense of urgency and panic.

key_points.script_text: Jonathan has been living on Saint Helena since 1882 and has become a local celebrity, appearing on the reverse of St Helena's 5p coin. The islanders are genuinely worried about his death, but thankfully, he was found alive.
key_points.visual_description: Photograph of Jonathan next to a BBC news page on an iPad, showing proof of life. The camera is positioned at a slight angle, creating a sense of intimacy and warmth. Soft focus and natural lighting create a cozy atmosphere. The pace is slow and gentle, building trust with the viewer.

key_points.script_text: Despite being blind and losing his sense of smell, Jonathan remains healthy and active, enjoying bananas and a strong libido. He has even tried to mate with two younger tortoises on the island.
key_points.visual_description: Footage of Jonathan grazing on grass in his enclosure, with the camera capturing the movement and texture of the environment. The lighting is natural and warm, highlighting the beauty of nature. The pace is slow and peaceful, showcasing Jonathan's vitality.

summary.script_text: The viral crypto death scam targeting Jonathan has been exposed, but the real impact on the community remains significant. The incident highlights the importance of verifying information and respecting the lives of others.
summary.visual_description: Aerial shot of Saint Helena island at sunset, with the governor's mansion and lush greenery in the foreground. The camera pans out to reveal the vast expanse of the ocean, symbolizing the global reach of the scam. Soft focus and warm lighting create a sense of reflection and contemplation. The pace is slow and thoughtful, encouraging the viewer to reflect on the incident.
